title: Amsterdam Neighbourhoods
standfirst: Amsterdam is more than just its famous canal ring. From the creative industrial hub of Noord to the leafy 19th-century elegance of De Plantage, each of our 10 districts has its own soul, its own history, and its own secrets.

products.title: De Plantage
products.description: Leafy gardens, Artis Zoo, and 19th-century elegance. Built as a 'pleasure garden' outside the city walls.

products.title: Amsterdam Oost
products.description: Indische Buurt, Oosterpark, and modern design. A vibrant mix of historic architecture and cutting-edge creative hubs.

products.title: De Wallen
products.description: The medieval heart of the city. Oude Kerk and hidden heritage inside Amsterdam's literal foundation.

products.title: Jordaan
products.description: Charming canals, brown cafés, and the iconic 9 Straatjes. Amsterdam's most celebrated neighbourhood.

products.title: De Pijp
products.description: The Latin Quarter. Famous for the Albert Cuyp Market and the city's most diverse food scene.

products.title: Amsterdam Noord
products.description: Industrial cool across the water. Home to NDSM wharf, street art, and the city's most experimental architecture.

products.title: Museumplein
products.description: The cultural heartbeat. Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh, and Stedelijk — three world-class museums on one lawn.

products.title: Centrum / Canal Ring
products.description: UNESCO Heritage. The golden age heart of Amsterdam with its iconic concentric canals.

products.title: Oud-West
products.description: Foodhallen and local vibes. A trendy residential area known for its craft coffee and independent shops.

products.title: Westerpark
products.description: Culture meets nature. A massive park centered around a repurposed 19th-century gasworks.
